design_plan: Python RNG Execution: seed = hash('Super vs Folk|competitor_comparison|people comparing Super with Folk|2026-07-06T07:08:33.996118+00:00') → selected hero architecture = comparison-lab. AIDA Check: Attention (wide editorial hero), Interest (comparison grid + proof), Desire (motion media + buyer guide), Action (CTA band). Font Selection: Cabinet Grotesk. Hero Math Verification: max-width 1120px, clamp headline for 2–3 lines. Bento Density Verification: compare-grid and bento use grid-auto-flow: dense. GSAP Motion Selection: image-scale-fade-scroll + hover-physics. Label Sweep & Button Check: all primary CTAs black pill, secondary white pill, contrast verified.
headline: Super vs Folk — personal AI agents for real computer work

site_brief.goal: Create a fair but persuasive competitor comparison page for Super vs Folk. Use supplied sources for current facts, avoid fake claims, and make the practical argument that Super is better suited for repeated computer-use work because its cache can lower repeated execution cost. Include a long-form buyer guide with a decision matrix, migration checklist, how-to evaluation plan, and source-backed caveats. Previous comparison-page failures omitted visible buyer-guide and decision-matrix language even when the article was useful; that must never happen. The visible page must include the exact phrases Buyer guide and Decision matrix.
site_brief.title: Super vs Folk

summary: A fair, practical comparison for people deciding between Super and Folk. Folk is useful for lightweight organization and niche automation. Super is purpose-built for durable computer-use workflows, with a reusable computer-use cache that makes repeated work faster and cheaper over time. This guide explains the market context, shows how to evaluate both tools hands-on, and helps you choose with a clear Buyer guide and Decision matrix.
